NCT03930732.name: BOREAS
NCT03930732.group: Moderate-severe COPD on triple therapy, blood eos >=300/uL, dupilumab 300 mg SC Q2W vs placebo (52 wk)

allOutcomes.title: Pre-bronchodilator FEV1 change at wk 12 (mL, MD)

NCT03930732.snippet: Source: Bhatt SP et al. N Engl J Med 2023;389:205-214 (BOREAS).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 939 adults with moderate-to-severe COPD on stable triple inhaled therapy (ICS + LABA + LAMA), post-bronchodilator FEV1 30-80% predicted, symptomatic (CAT >=10), current or former smokers, and blood eosinophils >=300/uL randomized 1:1 double-blind to dupilumab 300 mg SC every 2 weeks (n=468) or matching placebo (n=471) for 52 weeks.

evidence.label: Primary - Moderate/Severe Exacerbation Rate
evidence.text: Annualized rate of moderate/severe COPD exacerbations: dupilumab 0.78 vs placebo 1.10; rate ratio 0.70 (95% CI 0.58 to 0.86; P<0.001). 30% relative reduction. Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - FEV1, SGRQ, E-RS:COPD
evidence.text: Pre-bronchodilator FEV1 change from baseline to wk 12: dupilumab +83 mL vs placebo +23 mL; ETD +60 mL (95% CI +18 to +102). SGRQ change -9.8 vs -6.4 (ETD -3.4). E-RS:COPD total score improvement significant.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IRT randomization stratified by eosinophil count and smoking status. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo SC injection. D3 LOW - 93% completion. D4 LOW - central exacerbation adjudication. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 40 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria:~* Participants with a physician diagnosis of COPD who met the following criteria at screening:~  * Current or former smokers with a smoking history of ≥10 pack-years.~ * Moderate-to-severe COPD (post-bronchodilator FEV1/ forced vital capacity [FVC] ratio <0.70 and post-bronchodilator FEV1 % predicted >30% and ≤70%).~ * Medical Research Council (MRC) Dyspnea Scale grade ≥2.~ * Patient-reported history of signs and symptoms of chronic bronchitis (chronic productive cough) ...

NCT04456673.name: NOTUS
NCT04456673.group: Moderate-severe COPD on triple therapy, blood eos >=300/uL, dupilumab 300 mg SC Q2W vs placebo (52 wk)

allOutcomes.title: Pre-bronchodilator FEV1 change at wk 12 (mL, MD)

NCT04456673.snippet: Source: Bhatt SP et al. N Engl J Med 2024;390:2274-2286 (NOTUS).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 935 adults with moderate-to-severe COPD on triple therapy, symptomatic, current or former smokers, and blood eosinophils >=300/uL randomized 1:1 double-blind to dupilumab 300 mg SC every 2 weeks (n=468) or matching placebo (n=467) for 52 weeks. NOTUS was the replicate pivotal trial to BOREAS.

evidence.label: Primary - Moderate/Severe Exacerbation Rate
evidence.text: Annualized rate of moderate/severe COPD exacerbations: dupilumab 0.86 vs placebo 1.30; rate ratio 0.66 (95% CI 0.54 to 0.82; P<0.001). 34% relative reduction. Primary endpoint met, replicating BOREAS.

evidence.label: Secondary - FEV1 and SGRQ
evidence.text: Pre-bronchodilator FEV1 change from baseline to wk 12: dupilumab +82 mL vs placebo +43 mL; ETD +39 mL (95% CI -2 to +81). SGRQ improvement significant.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central randomization. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo. D3 LOW - 93% completion. D4 LOW - central exacerbation adjudication. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 40 Years; max age 85 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Participants with a physician diagnosis of COPD who met the following criteria at screening:~  * Current or former smokers with a smoking history of ≥10 pack-years.~ * Moderate-to-severe COPD (post-bronchodilator FEV1/ forced vital capacity [FVC] ratio <0.70 and post-bronchodilator FEV1 % predicted >30% and ≤70%).~ * Medical Research Council (MRC) Dyspnea Scale grade ≥2.~ * Patient-reported history of signs and symptoms of chronic bronchitis (chronic productive cough) ...

title: RapidMeta Respiratory | Dupilumab for COPD with Type 2 Inflammation v1.1
